channel.title: Haberdash Books
channel.description: The World's Forgotten Classics. Free audiobook readings of public domain masterpieces that history forgot. One chapter at a time.
channel.itunes:author: Haberdash Books
channel.itunes:summary: The World's Forgotten Classics. Free audiobook readings of public domain masterpieces that history forgot. One chapter at a time.

item.title: He Died Publishing His Masterpiece. Then the World Forgot.
item.description:
Book title: Tre croci (Three Crosses)
A psychological masterpiece of Italian realism, buried for a century until now—the story of three brothers whose desperation drives them toward a crime that will unravel their lives.
Giulio runs a failing bookshop in Siena. When financial ruin looms, he and his brothers turn to forgery. What begins as petty crime becomes a devastating exploration of shame, guilt, and the corrosive power of secrets. Federigo Tozzi, a master of psychological realism, dissects the human condition with surgical precision, offering readers a portrait of moral collapse as relevant today as when he wrote it in 1920—the year of his death.
This video contains the translator's foreword and chapter one. Find the complete audiobook at haberdashbooks.com
Narrated by AI voice in a calm British accent for relaxed listening.
CHAPTERS 0:00 — Introduction 0:21 — Haberdash Books 0:34 — About Federigo Tozzi 2:22 — Translator's Note 5:18 — Tre croci, Chapter 1 39:18 — More from Haberdash Books
🎧 Full audiobook: https://haberdashbooks.com 📚 Full catalogue: https://haberdashbooks.com/catalogue 📧 Free weekly forgotten classic: https://samus117.github.io/haberdash-podcast/subscribe.html ❤️ Support us on Patreon: https://patreon.com/haberdashbooks
#Audiobook #FreeAudiobook #PublicDomainAudiobook #ForgottenClassic #ClassicLiterature

item.title: He Wrote America's Greatest Frontier Epic. Then He Was Completely Forgotten.
item.description:
Book title: The Covered Wagon (The Covered Wagon)
Published in 1922 and adapted into a blockbuster silent film, Emerson Hough's masterpiece vanished from literary memory—despite changing American culture forever.
'The Covered Wagon' follows Jesse Wingate and four hundred families as they gather at the Missouri River in 1848, preparing for a perilous two-thousand-mile journey to Oregon. Through their eyes, we witness the hopes, conflicts, and desperate choices that defined westward expansion. But beneath the adventure lies a profound meditation on ambition, sacrifice, and the human cost of building a nation.
This video contains chapter one. Find the complete audiobook at haberdashbooks.com
Narrated by AI voice in a calm British accent for relaxed listening.
CHAPTERS 0:00 — Introduction 0:21 — Haberdash Books 0:34 — About Emerson Hough 2:00 — The Covered Wagon, Chapter 1 21:25 — More from Haberdash Books
🎧 Full audiobook: https://haberdashbooks.com 📚 Full catalogue: https://haberdashbooks.com/catalogue ❤️ Support us on Patreon: https://patreon.com/haberdashbooks
#Audiobook #FreeAudiobook #PublicDomainAudiobook #ForgottenClassic #Chapter1

item.title: Finland's Greatest Novelist Was Erased From History. We Found His Masterpiece.
item.description:
Book title: Rautatie (The Railway)
A Finnish literary giant vanished from English consciousness—but his masterpiece about progress, longing, and village life endures as proof of his genius.
In 1911, Juhani Aho crafted a novel about a railway arriving in a frozen Finnish village, where ordinary lives brush against extraordinary change. Through vivid prose and subtle observation, he explores how tradition and modernity collide, how a single moment reshapes a community forever. This is a story about the roads we take and those we leave behind—rich, atmospheric, deeply human.
This video contains the translator's foreword and chapter one. Find the complete audiobook at haberdashbooks.com
Narrated by AI voice in a calm British accent for relaxed listening.
CHAPTERS 0:00 — Introduction 0:23 — Haberdash Books 0:33 — About Juhani Aho 2:15 — Translator's Note 4:48 — Rautatie, Chapter 1 31:54 — More from Haberdash Books
